gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/106)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/106)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ فِي مَسْحِ الْخُفَّيْنِ تَقِيَّةٌ فَقَالَ ثَلَاثَةٌ لَا أَتَّقِي فِيهِنَّ أَحَداً شُرْبُ الْمُسْكِرِ وَ مَسْحُ الْخُفَّيْنِ وَ مُتْعَةُ الْحَجِّ
قَالَ زُرَارَةُ وَ لَمْ يَقُلْ الْوَاجِبُ عَلَيْكُمْ أَلَّا تَتَّقُوا فِيهِنَّ أَحَداً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad, from Hareyz, from Zurara who said,

‘I said to himasws, ‘With regards to the wiping (over) the two shoes, can there be dissimulation?’ So heasws said: ‘There are three (acts in religion), there should be no dissimulation with regards to these – one is drinking the wine, and wiping the two shoes/socks, and Tumatto of the Hajj’.
Zurara said, ‘And he<sup>asws</sup> did not say: ‘It is an Obligation upon you that you would not be acting in dissimulation with regards to these with anyone’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad from Hariz from Zurarah who has said the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about wiping for Wudu’ over one’s shoes because of Taqiyyah (fear). He (the Imam) said, ‘I do not practice Taqiyyah before anyone in three things: Drinking intoxicating liquor, wiping for Wudu’ over the shoes and in Mut’ah during Hajj.’ Zurarah has said that he (the Imam) did not say, ‘It is obligatory on you to disregard Taqiyyah before anyone.’”
